content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901            322-6441            322-6444

December 24, 1980

Mr. Anthony Saul Alperin
Deputy City Attorney
Office of the District Attorney
City Hall East
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Dear Mr. Alperin:

This is in response to your letter of December 9, 1980 requesting
confirmation of my previous telephone conversation with you regard-
ing the filing of campaign statements in connection with Board of
Education races and proposed amendments to the Los Angeles City
Charter affecting the Board of Education.

Government Code Section 84215, effective January 1, 1981, governs
the place where campaign statements will be filed. Specifically,
Section 84215(e) provides that:

"(e) City elected officers, candidates for city office,
their controlled committees and committees formed or
existing primarily to support or oppose candidates or
measures in one city shall file the original and one
copy with the clerk of the city. Such elected officers,
candidates and committees need not file with the clerk
of the county in which they are domiciled."

As stated in your letter, the Los Angeles City Charter includes "the
Members of the Board of Education" of the Los Angeles Unified School
District as "Officers" of the City of Los Angeles. Therefore, cam-
paign statements filed in connection with Board of Education races
and proposed amendments to the Charter for the City of Los Angeles
affecting the election, appointment or removal of Members of the
Board of Education should be filed pursuant to Section 84215(e) with
the Los Angeles City Clerk.

If you have any further questions, please feel free to call me.

Sincerely,

Lynn Montgomery,
Staff Services Analyst

OFFICE OF

CITY ATTORNEY
CITY HALL EAST
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

BURT PINES
CITY ATTORNEY

December 9, 1980


Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Prim:

     This letter is written on behalf of Rex E. Layton,
Los Angeles City Clerk, seeking advice pursuant to Government
Code Section 83114(b).

     The Political Reform Act currently provides that
campaign statements filed by persons holding offices "voted
upon wholly within one city", candidates for such offices and
committees supporting them be filed with the City Clerk of
the city in which the election takes place. Government Code
Section 81005(d). Where the offices voted upon lie both inside
and outside city boundaries but "in not more than the county",
such statements are to be filed with the county clerk (in Los
Angeles County with the Registrar-Recorder). Government Code
Section 81005(e).

     Effective January 1, 1981, the law will change.
Pursuant to Chapter 289, Statutes of 1980, Government Code
Section 84215 will govern the place where campaign statements
will be filed. In particular, Sections 84215(d) and (e) will
provide as follows:

     "(d) County elected officers, municipal
court judges, candidates for such offices, their
controlled committees and committees formed or
existing primarily to support or oppose candidates
or measures in any number of jurisdictions within
one county, other than those specified in subdivision
(e), shall file the original and one copy with the
clerk of the county.

Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2


     "(e) City elected officers, candidates for
city office, their controlled committees and
committees formed or existing primarily to support
or oppose candidates or measures in one city shall
file the original and one copy with the clerk of
the city.  Such elected officers, candidates, and
committees need not file with the clerk of the
county in which they are domiciled."

     Section 5 of the Los Angeles City Charter includes "the
Members of the Board of Education" of the Los Angeles Unified
School District as "officers" of the City of Los Angeles.  Section
6 provides that they shall be elected.

     Section 255.1 of the Charter divides the Unified School
District into seven election districts.  One such district lies
wholly within the City of Los Angeles; the other six districts
extend into territory outside the boundaries of the City.

     Pursuant to Government Code Section 81005, as currently
applicable, campaign statements filed in connection with Board of
Education elections have been filed with the Los Angeles County
Registrar-Recorder.  (One election district was formed in 1978.
No election has yet been held for the district that lies wholly
within the City.)

     Please advise us at your earliest opportunity whether,
beginning in 1981, campaign statements filed in connection with
Board of Education races (i.e., by incumbents, candidates, their
controlled committees, committees formed or existing primarily to
support them and "local general purpose" committees supporting or
opposing only such candidates and other candidates for City office)
should be filed with the Los Angeles City Clerk?

     Article IX, Section 16 of the California Constitution
requires the City of Los Angeles to submit proposed amendments to
its charter, affecting the election, appointment or removal of
members of the Board of Education, to the voters of both the City
and the Unified School District.  If campaign statements are filed
by committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose
such ballot measures, will Government Code Section 84215 require
those statements to be filed with the Los Angeles City Clerk or
the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder?

Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 3


     Having reviewed these issues and discussed them
briefly with your staff, we have tentatively concluded that
all of the statements described above should be filed with
the City Clerk.  However, because our conclusion is based in
part on advice from your staff, we would appreciate your advice
on the subject in writing.  Because we are in the process of
preparing materials for use in the upcoming City elections, we
would appreciate your response as soon as possible.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        BURT PINES, City Attorney

                                        By [signature]
                                           ANTHONY SAUL ALPARIN
                                           Deputy City Attorney

ASA:ams
(213) 485-4230

cc:  Rex E. Layton, City Clerk
     Ed Posorski, Deputy County Counsel

sections.question: Whether, beginning in 1981, campaign statements filed in connection with Board of Education races (i.e., by incumbents, candidates, their controlled committees, committees formed or existing primarily to support them and "local general purpose" committees supporting or opposing only such candidates and other candidates for City office) should be filed with the Los Angeles City Clerk? And if campaign statements are filed by committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose proposed amendments to the City Charter affecting the election, appointment or removal of members of the Board of Education, will Government Code Section 84215 require those statements to be filed with the Los Angeles City Clerk or the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder?

sections.question_synthetic: Should campaign statements for Los Angeles Board of Education races and proposed charter amendments affecting Board member election/appointment be filed with the City Clerk under new Government Code Section 84215 effective January 1, 1981, given that Board members are defined as City officers but elected in districts extending beyond city boundaries?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Campaign statements for Board of Education races and charter amendments affecting Board member election should be filed with the Los Angeles City Clerk under Government Code Section 84215(e), since Board members are designated as City officers under the Los Angeles Charter.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request from Los Angeles City Attorney (December 9, 1980) and the FPPC response letter (December 24, 1980). The extraction focuses on the FPPC response components. Minor OCR quality issues present but content is largely legible. The question and conclusion are clearly stated in the document.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff analyst responds to City Attorney's December 9, 1980 inquiry regarding filing locations for campaign statements under new Government Code Section 84215 effective January 1, 1981, confirming that Board of Education campaign statements should be filed with the City Clerk since Board members are designated as City officers.
